trivia_bullets.text: Deliberate design callback to Colossal Dreadmaw, the beloved vanilla 6/6 green dinosaur meme creature. Same mana cost ({4}{G}{G}), same stat boost (+6/+6), same trample. The card lets any creature 'be a Colossal Dreadmaw for the day.'
sources: Dexerto: 'Modern Horizons 3's Colossal Dreadmask turns iconic Creature into Equipment'

trivia_bullets.text: "No legs to quake, no lungs to bellow, but if you see its teeth, it's still too late."

Living weapon first appeared in Mirrodin Besieged (2011) to represent Phyrexian invasion flavor. Colossal Dreadmask uses the mechanic in MH3 in a non-Phyrexian, Eldrazi-horror context -- an unusual flavor departure for the keyword.
sources: Draftsim: 'All 19 Living Weapon Cards in Magic Ranked'

trivia_bullets.text: Community sentiment: Dexerto roundup quotes Reddit users calling it a WotC embrace of the Dreadmaw meme; 'Equipping this to a Dreadmaw would be peak Magic.' Draftsim's living weapon ranking places it at no. 10 of 19, describing it as a meme card with limited serious play outside niche commanders.
sources: Dexerto: 'Modern Horizons 3's Colossal Dreadmask turns iconic Creature into Equipment'
Draftsim: 'All 19 Living Weapon Cards in Magic Ranked'

flags.color_magic_tag_note: green-magic appears in tags_filter which is correct per BBL convention
flags.edgelord_flag: Colossal Dreadmask is the third MH3 artifact/equipment in inventory (with Inventor's Axe 126 and Unstable Amulet 142). Distinct from the energy pair -- its living-weapon mechanic and Dreadmaw-callback identity make it a meme-resonance anchor rather than an energy-economy piece. Could justify a separate lair angle (Dreadmaw-homage / big-green creatures) or fold into a broader MH3-artifacts cluster.
